code.description:
Code from the recovery email

If you want to submit a code, use this field, but make sure to _not_ include the email field, as well.

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

email.description:
The email address or phone number to verify

If the provided address belongs to a valid account, a verification email or SMS will be sent.

If you want to notify the email address if the account does not exist, see
the [notify_unknown_recipients flag](https://www.ory.com/docs/kratos/self-service/flows/verify-email-account-activation#attempted-verification-notifications)

If a code was already sent, including this field in the payload will invalidate the sent code and re-send a new code.

method.description:
Method is the method that should be used for this verification flow

Allowed values are `link` and `code`.
link VerificationStrategyLink
code VerificationStrategyCode

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks

link.description: Update Verification Flow with Link Method

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

email.description:
Email to Verify

Needs to be set when initiating the flow. If the email is a registered
verification email, a verification link will be sent. If the email is not known,
a email with details on what happened will be sent instead.

format: email

method.description:
Method is the method that should be used for this verification flow

Allowed values are `link` and `code`
link VerificationStrategyLink
code VerificationStrategyCode

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks
